lede: A look at June 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Abel Kirui and Deborah Compagnoni, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Terry Nichols
events.desc: Terry Nichols is sentenced to life in prison for his role in the Oklahoma City bombing.

events.title: Solidarity (Polish trade union)
events.desc: Solidarity's victory in the 1989 Polish legislative election occurs, the first election since the Communist Polish United Workers' Party abandoned its monopoly of power. It sparks off the Revolutions of 1989 in Eastern Europe.

events.title: Tonga
events.desc: Tonga gains independence from the British Empire.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: The Dunkirk evacuation ends: the British Armed Forces completes evacuation of 338,000 troops from Dunkirk in France. To rally the morale of the country, Winston Churchill delivers, only to the House of Commons, his famous "We shall fight on the beaches" speech.

events.title: Hungary
events.desc: Hungary loses 71% of its territory and 63% of its population when the Treaty of Trianon is signed in Paris.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Russia opens the Brusilov Offensive with an artillery barrage of Austro-Hungarian lines in Galicia.

events.title: Emily Davison
events.desc: Emily Davison, a suffragette, runs out in front of King George V's horse at The Derby. She is trampled, never regains consciousness, and dies four days later.

events.title: Massachusetts
events.desc: Massachusetts becomes the first state of the United States to set a minimum wage.

events.title: Louisiana
events.desc: Following Louisiana's admittance as a U.S. state, the Louisiana Territory is renamed the Missouri Territory.

events.title: Élisabeth Thible
events.desc: Élisabeth Thible becomes the first woman to fly in an untethered hot air balloon. Her flight covers four kilometres (2.5 mi) in 45 minutes, and reached an estimated 1,500 metres (4,900 ft) in altitude.

births.name: Angelina Jolie
births.line: American actress, filmmaker, humanitarian, and activist

births.name: Bronisław Komorowski
births.line: Polish historian and politician, 5th President of Poland

deaths.text: René Maurice Fréchet, French mathematician and academic
Reinhard Heydrich, German SS officer and a principle architect of the Holocaust
Emperor Huizong of Song, Emperor of China from 1100 to 1126
